original_hypothesis.title: Unexpected revert propagation can stall verification if SignalService syncChainData reverts inside verifyBlocks finalization
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: verifyBlocks performs external calls to SignalService.getSyncedChainData and potentially syncChainData after updating verifiedTransitionId for multiple blocks and then updating lastVerifiedBlockId at the end. A revert in SignalService calls will revert the whole verification transaction, preventing verification progress and bond returns, potentially halting the chain if SignalService is buggy/misconfigured or becomes temporarily unavailable.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a realistic liveness risk because SignalService is an external upgradeable dependency. Even if trusted, bugs or config mistakes can cause reverts. The call is in the critical path of block finalization.
deployment_assumptions: Resolved 'signal_service' can revert on getSyncedChainData or syncChainData under some conditions (e.g., authorization, paused state, bad config)
blockSyncThreshold is non-zero so sync is attempted
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: DeployOnL1.s.sol authorizes taikoL1 on SignalService conditionally; if not authorized or later deauthorized, syncChainData may revert. SignalService ownership is transferred to timelock.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _syncChainData calls ISignalService.getSyncedChainData and syncChainData without try/catch; revert aborts verification

preconditions: signal_service address is set and is callable
SignalService call can revert (auth, paused, internal bug, or misconfiguration)
Verification relies on syncChainData being executed in same transaction (no alternative path)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: external dependency revert in critical path
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: chain verification progress coupled to cross-chain sync
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no try/catch or deferred syncing mechanism

analysis_questions: Does SignalService.syncChainData have authorization checks that could fail after deployment changes?
Can _config.blockSyncThreshold be zeroed to disable syncing (it is hard-coded non-zero here: 16)?
Is syncing strictly required for correctness, or can it be made best-effort?
validation_requirements: Review SignalService implementation for revert conditions
Simulate deauthorization/paused SignalService and attempt verifyBlocks
Consider whether a revert would also revert bond transfers and verifiedTransitionId updates (it would), causing liveness halt

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If signal_service becomes misconfigured or reverts, any call to verifyBlocks reverts, preventing lastVerifiedBlockId from advancing and blocking finalization and bond returns until fixed.
prerequisites: SignalService misconfiguration or bug (or admin action), or dependency outage

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Indirect but high: halts verification and delays/blocks bond returns; may stall rollup finality
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users relying on finality and provers awaiting payouts
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Admin can fix config/upgrade SignalService; downtime until intervention

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests ensuring verifyBlocks succeeds when signal_service sync fails or is disabled

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor verifyBlocks reverts correlated with SignalService state

validation_result.mechanism: unguarded external call in critical verification path
validation_result.impact_summary: A revert in SignalService.getSyncedChainData or syncChainData will revert the entire verifyBlocks transaction, preventing lastVerifiedBlockId from advancing, blocking bond returns, and eventually stalling proposals when the ring buffer fills.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The _syncChainData function in LibVerifying.sol makes two external calls to SignalService without any try/catch error handling. First, getSyncedChainData is called unconditionally (regardless of blockSyncThreshold). Second, syncChainData is called conditionally based on the sync gap. Both calls are made after the verification loop has completed but within the same transaction that updates lastVerifiedBlockId. If either call reverts, the entire transaction reverts, rolling back ALL state changes including the verified block updates and bond transfers. Since verifyBlocks is called from proposeBlock, proveBlock, and the standalone verifyBlocks entry point, ALL verification paths are affected. There is no alternative code path to advance verification without triggering the sync logic.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is clearly visible in LibVerifying.sol. The _syncChainData function (lines ~190-210) makes raw external calls to SignalService without try/catch. The call to getSyncedChainData happens unconditionally every time numBlocksVerified > 0, and the call to syncChainData happens when the sync gap exceeds blockSyncThreshold (16). The SignalService is resolved with allowZeroAddress=false, meaning if the address is not set, it reverts at resolution. If it IS set but the contract is paused, deauthorized, or buggy, the external call itself reverts.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Both getSyncedChainData and syncChainData are called without try/catch. Any revert from SignalService (due to authorization failure, paused state, or internal bug) will revert the entire verifyBlocks transaction, preventing verification progress.

The state update to lastVerifiedBlockId and the _syncChainData call are in the same transaction scope. A revert in _syncChainData rolls back the lastVerifiedBlockId update, meaning no verification progress can be made while SignalService is malfunctioning.

calling_sequence: LibVerifying.verifyBlocks iterates through blocks, updates blk.verifiedTransitionId, transfers bonds via tko.transfer
After loop: if numBlocksVerified > 0, sets _state.slotB.lastVerifiedBlockId = lastVerifiedBlockId
_syncChainData resolves 'signal_service' with allowZeroAddress=false (reverts if not set)
_syncChainData calls signalService.getSyncedChainData (unconditional external call, no try/catch)
If either external call reverts, the entire verifyBlocks transaction reverts
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical path is: verifyBlocks -> loop updates state -> _state.slotB.lastVerifiedBlockId = lastVerifiedBlockId -> _syncChainData -> signalService.getSyncedChainData() [REVERT HERE] -> entire tx reverts, rolling back lastVerifiedBlockId and all bond transfers. This means the same blocks will need to be re-verified, but they will hit the same revert every time, creating a permanent stall until SignalService is fixed.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Resolved 'signal_service' can revert on getSyncedChainData or syncChainData under some conditions
blockSyncThreshold is non-zero so sync is attempted
config_validation.deployment_evidence: DeployOnL1.s.sol shows: (1) SignalService authorization of taikoL1 is conditional: `if (vm.envAddress("SHARED_ADDRESS_MANAGER") == address(0)) { SignalService(signalServiceAddr).authorize(taikoL1Addr, true); }` - if SHARED_ADDRESS_MANAGER is non-zero, authorization is skipped. (2) SignalService ownership is transferred to timelock: `signalService.transferOwnership(timelock)` meaning future deauthorization or pause requires timelock action. (3) The blockSyncThreshold is hardcoded to 16 in TaikoL1.getConfig(). (4) getSyncedChainData is called unconditionally (before the threshold check), so even with threshold=0, this view call would still execute.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The scenario is realistic and does not require an attacker. A misconfiguration, admin action (deauthorizing TaikoL1 from SignalService), or a bug in an upgraded SignalService contract would trigger this. Specifically: (1) If SignalService is paused by its owner (timelock), syncChainData or getSyncedChainData may revert. (2) If TaikoL1 is deauthorized from SignalService, syncChainData will likely revert on the authorization check. (3) If SignalService is upgraded to a buggy version, either call could revert. In all cases, verifyBlocks becomes permanently non-functional until the issue is resolved. This blocks bond returns and eventually blocks new proposals when the ring buffer fills (numBlocks >= lastVerifiedBlockId + blockMaxProposals + 1).
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH impact on protocol liveness: (1) No blocks can be verified while SignalService is malfunctioning. (2) Prover bonds (validityBond + livenessBond) cannot be returned, locking TKO funds. (3) When ring buffer fills after 864,000 unverified blocks, proposeBlock also fails, completely halting the L2 chain. (4) Cross-chain state sync stops, affecting bridge security. Recovery requires admin intervention through the timelock (7-day delay for non-admin-role holders) to fix SignalService configuration.
recommended_mitigations: Wrap _syncChainData's external calls in try/catch so verification can proceed even if syncing fails: try signalService.getSyncedChainData(...) returns (uint64 lastSyncedBlock, bytes32) { ... } catch { return; }
Make chain data syncing a separate, independent function that can be called optionally after verification, decoupling verification progress from sync success
Add a circuit breaker: if blockSyncThreshold is 0, skip _syncChainData entirely (the config comment says 'a value of zero disables syncing' but the code doesn't implement this)
Consider emitting an event when sync fails rather than silently proceeding, to alert operators
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy TaikoL1 and SignalService in a test environment. 2. Propose and prove a block. 3. Pause SignalService (or deauthorize TaikoL1 from it). 4. Attempt to call verifyBlocks - observe revert. 5. Verify that lastVerifiedBlockId has not advanced. 6. Unpause/reauthorize and verify blocks successfully.
uncertainties: The exact revert conditions of SignalService's getSyncedChainData and syncChainData depend on the SignalService implementation which is not fully in scope
Whether getSyncedChainData (a view function) can revert depends on SignalService internals - most view functions can still revert on invalid storage state or require statements
additional_testing_needed: Test verifyBlocks behavior when SignalService.getSyncedChainData reverts
Test verifyBlocks behavior when SignalService.syncChainData reverts due to deauthorization
Test the cascading effect on proposeBlock and proveBlock when verification is stalled
Measure the time window for admin recovery via timelock when SignalService is misconfigured
